title: Pluggable fiber loopback apparatus and methods for fiber optical systems
abstract: The loopback apparatus disclosed herein is used with an optical fiber system having an optical fiber cable. The loopback apparatus includes an optical fiber having input and output ends and an output optical fiber having input and output ends. The loopback apparatus also includes an optical system that defines an optical path and that is configured to optically couple the output end of the input optical fiber with the input end of the output optical fiber over the optical path. The loopback apparatus also includes a thin-film filter disposed in the optical path and configured to provide a select amount of attenuation for light traveling over the optical path. The loopback apparatus can be plugged into and unplugged from the optical fiber cable. Loopback methods for measuring the performance of the optical fiber system using the loopback apparatus are also disclosed.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1. A loopback apparatus for use with a fiber optical system, comprising: at least one input optical fiber having input and output ends; at least one output optical fiber having input and output ends; an optical system that defines an optical path and that is configured to optically couple the output end of the at least one input optical fiber with the input end of the at least one output optical fiber over the optical path; and a thin-film filter (TFF) disposed in the optical path and configured to provide a select amount of attenuation for light traveling over the optical path. 2. The loopback apparatus according to claim 1, wherein the optical system comprises first and second optical fiber collimators each having front ends, with the first and second collimators disposed along an optical axis with their respective front ends confronting, and wherein the TFF is disposed between the confronting front ends. 3. The loopback apparatus according to any of claims 1-2, wherein the optical system consists of a single optical fiber collimator and wherein the TFF is reflective. 4. The loopback apparatus according to any of claims 1-3, wherein the at least one input optical fiber comprises N multiple input optical fibers and wherein the at least one output optical fiber comprises N multiple output optical fibers. 5. The loopback apparatus according to any of claims 1-4, wherein the TFF comprises a tap filter. 6. The loopback apparatus according to any of claims 1-5, wherein the optical system comprises at least one collimator that includes a gradient-index (GRIN) lens having an end face, and wherein the TFF is formed on the end face. 7. The loopback apparatus according to claim 6, wherein the at least one input optical fiber and the at least one output optical fiber each includes respective front-end sections supported by an optical fiber support member that resides in contact with or proximate to the GRIN lens on a side opposite the end face of the GRIN lens. 8. The loopback apparatus according to any of claims 1-7, wherein the input end of the at least one input optical fiber and the output end of the at least one output optical fiber are connectorized using at least one first connector. 9. The loopback apparatus according to claim 8, wherein the fiber optical system includes an optical fiber cable that includes at least one transmit optical fiber and at least one receive optical fiber, wherein the at least one transmit optical fiber and at least one receive optical fiber are terminated by at least one second connector, and further comprising: an adapter configured to operably receive the at least one first connector and the at least one second connectors to place the at least one transmit optical fiber in optical communication with the at least one input optical fiber and the at one receive optical fiber in optical communication with the at least one output optical fiber. 10. A fiber optical system, comprising: optical equipment having a transmitter and a receiver; an optical fiber cable that includes at least one transmit optical fiber optically coupled to the transmitter and at least one receive optical fiber optically coupled to the receiver; a loopback apparatus comprising: at least one input optical fiber optically coupled to the at least one transmit optical fiber; at least one output optical fiber optically coupled to the at least one receive optical fiber; an optical system configured to optically couple the at least one input optical fiber to the at least one output optical fiber over an optical path; and 

 a thin-film filter operably disposed in the optical path and configured to provide a select amount of optical attenuation. 11. The fiber optical system according to claim 10, wherein the optical system comprises first and second optical fiber collimators each having front ends, with the first and second collimators disposed along an optical axis with their respective front ends confronting, and wherein the TFF is disposed between the confronting front ends. 12. The fiber optical system according to any of claims 10-11, wherein the optical system consists of a single optical fiber collimator and wherein the TFF is reflective. 13. The fiber optical system according to any of claims 10-12, wherein: the at least one input optical fiber comprises N multiple input optical fibers and wherein the at least one output optical fiber comprises N multiple output optical fibers. 14. The fiber optical system according to any of claims 10-13, wherein the optical system comprises at least one collimator that includes a gradient-index (GRIN) lens having an end face, and wherein the TFF is formed on the end face. 15. The fiber optical system according to claim 14, wherein the at least one input optical fiber and the at least one output optical fiber each includes respective front-end sections supported by an optical fiber support member that resides in contact with or proximate to the GRIN lens on a side opposite the end face of the GRIN lens. 16. The fiber optical system according to any of claims 10-15, wherein the input end of the at least one input optical fiber and the output end of the at least one output optical fiber are connectorized using at least one first connector. 17. A method of evaluating a performance of a fiber optical system that includes optical equipment and an optical fiber cable that supports at least one transmit optical fiber and at least one receive optical fiber, comprising: 

 transmitting, from a transmitter in the optical equipment, optical signals over the at least one transmit optical fiber to at least one input optical fiber of a loopback apparatus; sending the optical signals from the at least one input optical fiber to at least one output optical fiber of the loop back apparatus over an optical path, including directing the optical signals to a thin-film filter that resides in the optical path and that is configured to perform filtering of the optical signals to form filtered optical signals; directing the filtered optical signals from the at least one output optical fiber to the at least one receive optical fiber of the optical fiber cable and then to at least one receiver in the optical equipment; and converting the filtered optical signals received by the at least one receiver to electrical signals and then processing the electrical signals. 18. The method according to claim 17, wherein the optical path is defined by an optical system that comprises at least one optical collimator. 19. The method according to claim 18, wherein the at least one optical collimator comprises confronting input and output collimators, and wherein the thin-film filter resides between the confronting input and output collimators, and wherein the optical signals are transmitted through the thin-film filter. 20. The method according to claim 18, wherein the at least one optical collimator comprises a single optical collimator having an end face, and wherein the thin-film filter is operably disposed in contact with or proximate to the end face, and wherein the thin-film filter is reflective so that the optical signals reflect from the thin-film filter. 21. The method according to any of claims 17-20, wherein the filtering of the optical signals comprises power filtering the optical signals. 22. The method according to any of claims 17-21, wherein the filtering of the optical signals comprises spectral filtering of the optical signals.
